---
title: "About Me"
---

## Education

I will be completing my Bachelor's degree in Environmental studies this March after 3 and half years at UCSB. Throughout my undergraduate experience I did take the opportunity to study abroad this past summer in French Polynesia where we stayed on the islands of Tahiti and Moor-ea for a total of 6 weeks completing various field studies and learning about the society islands rich history.

I have taken many formative classes during my time learning about a range of topics from the physics, chemistry, and biology, to environmental justice and what building sustainable communities looks like in the present time. Below are some specific classes that I have felt helped the most with professional my development as I begin my search for a full time position.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Courses

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Statistics

ENVS 193 DS - Environmental Data Science

PSTAT 5LS - Statistics for Life Sciences

EEMB 146 - Biometery (Biological statistics)

EEMB 148 - Stream Ecology

### Spatial Modeling

ENVS 154 - GIS for Environmental Applications

### Conservation Managment

ENVS 130C - Aquatic Food Systems

ENVS 140 - Wildfire Ecology

ENVS 103A - California Flora and Vegitation Identification

ENVS 163A - Water Supply and Demand

WRIT 105SW - Scientific Writing for the Public

## Professional Development

While attending UCSB I have been fortunate enough to participate in a number of internships that have shaped my experience and the type work that I am looking for in the future.

I have spent the last 3 years working in the LEAF lab participating in a number of field experiences that are shown in greater depth in the [Field Experiences](Experience.qmd) section.

I have also worked with The Nature Conservancy offering field trips on [Dangermond Preserve](https://www.nature.org/en-us/about-us/where-we-work/united-states/california/stories-in-california/dangermond-preserve/?en_txn1=p_gr.gd.eg.x.storiesandnews.dynamic.gh&gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=23483423232&gbraid=0AAAAADwfaooMMPhFaJXwy-SRR1Nb_5wXw&gclid=CjwKCAiAzZ_NBhAEEiwAMtqKy3anIz59Y6vZDicyOjsm05aLQ9PXL-63RwhVbx-7hPstQfYfXd0F6BoC390QAvD_BwE) to students across the Lompoc school district teaching about the local Oakwoodland ecosystem. Most recently I have been working with UCSB's Cheadle Center for Biodiversity and Ecological Restoration (CCBER) to give education walks to preschool students at both on campus child care centers.

::: {style="text-align: center;"}
![](IMG_3990.png){.photo-border width="45%"} ![](IMG_2877.jpg){.photo-border width="45%"}
:::

### Hobbies

When I'm not in nature with students I still love to be there with myself and my friends.

Most of my hobbies revolve around the great outdoors and include but are not limited to biking, climbing and

::: {style="text-align: center;"}
![](IMG_2849.jpg){.photo-border width="45%"} ![](climbing%20.jpg){.photo-border width="45%"}
:::

backpacking!

::: {style="text-align: center;"}
![](backpacking.jpg){.photo-border width="45%"} ![](sequoia.jpg){.photo-border}
:::
